mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS year-over-year growth is positive but SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes a broader economic slowdown and reduced corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, but SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is high and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is also elevated, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy environment that constrains economic growth and corporate valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and CPIAUCSL annual change > 3.0%, but SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such periods over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic expansion, robust industrial production growth indicates healthy corporate activity and demand, which supports higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth is positive during an expansion, but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year rolling window within that expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A significant slowdown in non-farm payrolls growth indicates a weakening labor market and potential economic deceleration, which typically leads to reduced consumer spending and lower corporate earnings, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month average growth falls below 50,000, but SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 45% of such instances over a 7-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects strong underlying economic demand and investment, which acts as a positive leading indicator for broader economic activity and corporate profits, supporting S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows consistent positive year-over-year growth, but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is low and GDP growth (GDPC1) is also strong, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy supporting a robust economy, which provides a favorable environment for equity markets and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS < 2.0% and GDPC1 annual growth > 2.5%, but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 35% of such periods over a 7-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production signals a contraction in the manufacturing sector and overall supply-side weakness, which typically leads to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 1.0% over 3 months, but SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances over a 10-year rolling window.
